region_of_origin: Turkey / Middle East (fusion with South Asian tandoori technique)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for Chicken Tandoori Pizza; closest parent is pizza food product (FOODON_00002572).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pizza) used as closest parent; no specific code exists for Chicken Tandoori Pizza variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for Chicken Tandoori Pizza.

main_ingredients: tandoori marinade (yogurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili, garam masala)

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Chicken Tandoori Pizza as typically served at a Turkish or Middle Eastern restaurant, weighing approximately 300 g.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza with meat topping, baked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21299) and composite estimation from USDA Foundation Foods chicken breast (grilled, spiced) and pizza dough components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Chicken Tandoori Pizza in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Values estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping (NDB 21299, ~266 kcal/100g) with adjustments for tandoori-spiced chicken topping (higher protein, moderate fat). Macros cross-checked against USDA Foundation Foods grilled chicken breast and standard cheese pizza entries. Estimate is conservative and reflects a typical restaurant-prepared product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza and mixed wheat-flour baked products with meat toppings — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Chicken Tandoori Pizza. GI estimated at 60 based on analogous pizza with meat topping entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (pizza, cheese and tomato GI ~60; pizza with meat toppings typically 55–65). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 82.5) / 100 = 49.5, rounded to 49.7 accounting for serving size of 300 g (27.5 g carb/100g × 3 = 82.5 g carb per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~560 mg/100g; ~1680 mg per whole pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Chicken Tandoori Pizza due to its high sodium content from cheese, tomato sauce, and spice blends; a single serving can provide more than 70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~60) and high glycemic load per serving (~49.7) from refined wheat dough base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Chicken Tandoori Pizza in small portions due to its medium GI and high per-serving glycemic load driven by the refined flour crust; pairing with a salad and choosing a thin-crust variant can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese (~3.8 g/100g) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Chicken Tandoori Pizza intake as the combination of saturated fat from mozzarella cheese and high sodium content may adversely affect blood pressure and LDL cholesterol levels; occasional consumption in small portions is preferable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Tandoori Pizza is a fusion dish combining the South Asian tandoori cooking tradition with the Italian pizza format, commonly served at Turkish and Middle Eastern restaurants in Singapore and internationally. The pizza base is a leavened wheat-flour dough, baked until crisp, and topped with tomato sauce, mozzarella cheese, and chicken pieces marinated in a tandoori spice blend of yogurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili, and garam masala. The chicken is typically pre-cooked or grilled before being placed on the pizza, imparting a smoky, mildly spiced flavour with a tender texture. The dish is characterised by its vibrant orange-red hue from the tandoori marinade, a savoury and mildly spicy taste profile, and a combination of chewy dough, melted cheese, and juicy spiced chicken. Nutritionally, it provides moderate protein (~13.5 g/100g) from chicken and cheese, moderate carbohydrates (~27.5 g/100g) from the dough base, and moderate fat (~9.8 g/100g) including saturated fat from cheese. Sodium content is elevated (~560 mg/100g), making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium), and the glycemic load per full serving is high, requiring caution for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is halal-suitable when prepared with halal-certified chicken and ingredients, making it popular across diverse communities in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken tandoori pizza?
Can diabetics eat chicken tandoori pizza?
Chicken tandoori pizza GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Chicken Tandoori Pizza found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping data with adjustments for tandoori-spiced chicken. GI estimated from analogous pizza entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza with meat topping (NDB 21299) and USDA Foundation Foods grilled chicken breast, adjusted for tandoori spice marinade contribution. No exact match found in primary regional sources (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN). Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza composite source used for estimation. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza composite source used for estimation. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza composite source used for estimation. The food is classified under Turkish cuisine as served at Sultan Palace (Boat Quay, Singapore), though the dish itself is a fusion of Turkish pizza tradition and South Asian tandoori technique. Halal status is assumed based on restaurant context (Turkish/Lebanese cuisine restaurant) but should be verified against restaurant-specific halal certification. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate; a direct measurement study for this specific product does not exist in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (60 × 82.5 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving) / 100 = 49.5, reported as 49.7 with minor rounding adjustment.
